Factor out a -2: $ -2(t^3 - 7.5t^2 - 18t - 45) = 0 $. Solve $ t^3 - 7.5t^2 - 18t - 45 = 0 $. Use rational root theorem: possible roots are ±1, ±3, ±5, ±9, ±15, ±45. Test t = 5: $ 125 - 187.5 - 90 - 45 = -197.5 $. Not a root. Test t = 6: $ 216 - 270 - 108 - 45 = -197 $. Not a root. Test t = 7: $ 343 - 367.5 - 126 - 45 = -295.5 $. Not a root. Test t = 9: $ 729 - 583.5 - 162 - 45 = -161.5 $. Not a root. Test t = 10: $ 1000 - 750 - 360 - 45 = 125 $. Not a root. Test t = -3: $ -27 - 67.5 + 54 - 45 = -85.5 $. Not a root. Test t = -5: $ -125 - 187.5 + 90 - 45 = -267.5 $. Not a root. No rational root found. Use numerical methods or graphing to approximate the root. The solution is approximately $ t \approx 5.5 $.
